concerns.text: Task T060 mandates implementing a protocol to compare model error against 'known experimental values from literature'. This directly violates spec.md Assumptions which state: 'Physical measurement validation is out of scope for this computational feature; validation will use QM9 quantum calculation reference data as the ground truth standard'. The task silently relaxes the scope boundary defined in the spec, introducing work explicitly marked as 'downstream research requirement'.

concerns.location: tasks.md:Phase 3, T022; Phase 4, T035-T038; Phase 5, T046-T047
concerns.text: Tasks T022, T035, T036, T037, T038, T046, and T047 implement features (physical bond descriptors, peptide planarity, physics-informed loss, stereoisomer stratification, physical correlation) that are not present in spec.md as Functional Requirements or Success Criteria. While labeled as 'extensions' or 'addressing reviews', they constitute scope creep that violates Constitution Principle IV (Single Source of Truth) by authorizing work not defined in the spec.

Task T016 describes creating a '10k random subset... for initial prototyping and testing'. Spec.md User Story 1 and FR-001 mandate a '10k subset' as the final deliverable. The task's phrasing implies a temporary prototype, creating ambiguity about whether a second, final extraction is required or if the prototype satisfies the FR. This risks a silent weakening of the deliverable definition.

Task T043 includes a hard-coded URL (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/P-value) and Wikidata ID within the task description. While the spec requires statistical tests, embedding unverified secondary sources (Wikipedia) in the task definition violates Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) which mandates citations be verified against primary sources. The task should reference the method (paired t-test) without hard-coding unverified URLs.
